23
24
25
26 Man Tax: Francois Civil
"When we engage in what we are naturally suited to do, our work takes on the quality of play and it is play that stimulates creativity." - Linda Naiman So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, **Imgur Creator Spotlight is now in your hands!! Having trouble finding people I haven't already featured recently. Now I'm going to just do who YOU nominate. Tag me or DM me and I'll feature whoever you pick.**
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: 30 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 258
item.description:
Polar bears found living together in an abandoned weather station https://www.dmitrykokh.com/abandoned
Dog’s reaction when he realises his sister was adopted into the same home.
A very small choir with very strong opinions. ~u/Sad-Fishing9705
In Sana’a, Yemen, a 14-year-old boy named Ridan Al-Mashouly began regularly helping an elderly homeless man living in his community.
Custard and Cream are sisters and best friends. They’re never far from each other! ~u/Modern-Moo
At Sao Vicente de Paulo School, 5 years old Shaquela De Xavier was seen helping her classmate Rivaldo Sequeira eat lunch.
My baby CaliMy Kitten ~u/JoshB66_
item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: Trump to force taxpayers to pay reparations to Jan 6 insurrectionists.
item.description: FP: I am being crushed by depression. dragged by anxiety. And far passed pissed off because of PTSD. It sucks knowing that I'll never get the health care or support I need. I'm tired of just scraping by and having to beg for help. I can't keep doing this. But when I watched Afghanistan fall on TV. I knew everything that I thought I did meant nothing. And all the pain and all the suffering that I would continue to feel was for nothing. And now I'm watching the standards I was broken upon as a child all the way through the military and judged upon afterwards. I never wanted to join the Army. It was either that or be homeless. It was 2008. There was no economy. Graduated high school with no family support. I really wish I had died over there. So I wouldn't have had to see all of this. This article has affected me. Like when watching Afghanistan fall... All this physical pain, all this mental torment was for nothing. The alleged child-raping president, Donald J. Trump, is trying to secure funding that will give reparations to everybody, including himself, involved in January 6th. Might have reminded you that January 6th was a violent insurrection that resulted in loss of life. I'm a disabled combat veteran with a terminally ill spouse. Since Trump has been in office, I've had to move across the country to try to find a better living situation. I'm having to pay for my wife's medications out of pocket, including some of my own medications out of pocket that are no longer covered by Veterans Affairs or state insurance. A nuisance of personal pride has died because I've had to ask communities like this one for help more times than I can count. People like me should not have to rely on wonderful people like you. My government should take care of this. But instead, we're giving the worst people in the country racist bigots who think the South or something will rise again. Where is the outrage about this? Where are the no kings protests? Why aren't y'all mad? (But seriously, a heartfelt thank you for the aid and motivation keeping my wife and I surviving. We are doing the best we can just like everybody else keeping our heads down.) https://linktr.ee/GridSquares
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.title: I'll have a satisfying rainy Sunday in the kitchen. Dump of Come taste some things !
item.description:
Man Makes Kebabs.
Ham and egg toast in Korean Style.
Cutting of Japanese noodles called Soba. When cutting this type of noodle, the edge of the dough can stretch slightly, so they trim that part off first. Otherwise, it can catch on the knife and stretch even more.
Japan’s Spicy Ramen.
Making a giant sesame dumpling.
Homemade Waffle Cones.
Sweet treat made to order.
Fresh Wafer Production.
Perfectly Even Slices...
Doner Kebab from Berlin.
Some useful tips.
It looks cool.
Creamy chicken patties, with made oil, inion, salt, chiken mince, black pepper, chili flake, cream, green onion. samosa pati cheese, water.
This is the correct amount of onion.
A perfect Flan.
Creamy homemade chocolate mousse.
Homemade Cheez-its.
Smash burger patty spacer and press.
Margherita-style cheese ramen
How to poach eggs the easy way.
POV of a busy chef.
Savory Meat Baklava. Crispy layers & tender beef.
Japanese Shokupan- the fluffiest white bread you'll ever try.
Snacks for movies.
Quick Snack. Or breakfast in Canary Islands.
Burrito gigante.
Commercial Stainless Steel Vegetable Chopper.
Rib puller
Lasagna from Rocco’s of Roc Beach in Queens. That's tooooo much.
Why the egg yolk ?

item.title: The REAL She-ro we all need but don't deserve
item.description: COLLIERVILLE MOM DETAINED AFTER ALLEGEDLY OPENING “SIDELINE SANGRIA STATION” AT YOUTH SOCCER GAME COLLIERVILLE, TN — Parents arriving at Rose Soccer Complex Saturday morning expecting Capri Suns and awkward small talk instead found what witnesses described as “a full-blown brunch winery experience with shin guards.” 38-year-old Brittany Calloway was reportedly escorted from a U-9 girls soccer game after allegedly setting up a folding patio bar beside Field 3 and selling “small batch mom juice” out of a monogrammed cooler during the first quarter. According to witnesses, Brittany arrived at approximately 8:12 a.m. wearing oversized sunglasses, a floppy beach hat, and a shirt that read “IT’S CALLED SELF-CARE, KEVIN.” She allegedly unloaded a collapsible table, two fake ferns, and a handwritten drink menu from the back of a white Tahoe with a “Powered By Pinot” sticker on the rear window. Authorities say the menu included: • “Collierville Cabernet-ish” — $9 • “Minivan Moscato” — $11 • “Pinot Grigio & Childhood Trauma” — $13 • “Ref Whistle Riesling” — $15 • A complimentary refill for any parent whose child got put in as goalie “for character development” Police say Brittany also had: a battery-powered blender, a ring light, a Venmo QR code taped to a pumpkin spice candle, and a Bluetooth speaker blasting early-2000s Kelly Clarkson on repeat. One father told officers, “I honestly thought she was part of the tournament sponsorship.” Another parent reportedly became concerned after her daughter asked, “Why does Chloe’s mom have a happy hour menu next to the orange slices?” When approached by officers, Brittany allegedly insisted she “wasn’t technically selling alcohol” and claimed parents were simply “donating toward emotional recovery.” “She was incredibly committed to the theme,” one officer stated. “She had punch cards. She offered us a loyalty program called ‘Sip Happens.’” Witnesses say Brittany remained calm while being escorted away, though she allegedly attempted to hand out one final mason jar labeled “Silent Carpool Sauvignon.” Despite the interruption, the Collierville Lady Fireballs went on to win 5-2, although several parents admitted they had absolutely no idea what the score was by halftime...
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: The Tiniest Snack
item.description: Sound on please Note- The Midwest red squirrel, even without a microphone shoved close to it, grumbles and makes a lot of noise at almost every moment it is awake. especially when they are eating Wildlife photographer Dani Connor records the sound of a 7 week old baby red squirrel makes while eating. FP Edit: Just watch the squirrel. Everything will be alright.
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.title: Feral funnies
item.description:
Welcome to this dump of feral memes. Sometimes I make the memes themed, sometimes I don't. If you enjoy laughing without talking politics and escaping reality, you’ll love following this page!
Snooty for a trash panda
I needs them
I don't even need a microdose. I just like snacks
Steal and use as you see fit
Somehow, technology never works for me but my IT guy hubby gets things to work simply by touching it
Hiss I am strong
Who among us does not find ecstacy in whipped cream?
Life is making me look old
A tiny cute baby demon
Nature is beautiful
877 cash now
True friends know how to communicate
I too, like raw piano
I feel the need for speed
Aww
I usually say swamp monster
I think she's adorable
Plus it's yummy
Very feral
Why didn't you believe them?!
Just a little treat for surviving the day
Nah, he's a sweetie
What the fuck
Me every day
I tell this to my kids most days but they still test the limit
Let's see what he has to say
Omg! It's the consequences of my actions
Damn it
Aww
Here to give you one item you accidentally threw in the trash
For reals
Die young
Send to your bestie
Me no likey
Don't know the artist but it's super cute
item.dc:creator: GigglySprinkleCupcake

item.title: 16 years
item.description: We have 3 beautiful girls, fortunately we split amicably but there is so much to think about. A shared house, car, obviously the kids. If I have to divorce someone I’m glad it’s her as we both want what’s best for the girls. We split in the fall and hadn’t had any kind of intimacy for a year at that point. Neither of us wanted to teach our kids that it’s okay to stay with someone if they just don’t make you happy anymore. I’m finally starting to let go of the life that was and starting to see her as more of a friend but I want to tell the story even if it’s just to get it for my chest. We both hurt each other. I lied to her. A lot. It was always about something small but all those lies add up. I’m a really great dad but maybe wasn’t a great husband to her. In my defence her standards were unachievable for me and she can be quite volatile , hence all the lying to keep things smooth. I was always afraid to tell her the truth when I knew she wouldn’t like it. I should never have chosen that route but I did and now I’m paying the consequences. She cheated on me. This is a tough one for me to talk about. In the fall of 2021 at the height of the pandemic, she cheated on me with the father of one of our kids classmates. It rocked me to my core, I was a complete mess for 6 months and honestly I’m still recovering. Maybe it makes me lame by today’s standards but cheating in a relationship is one of my biggest hangups and I really thought she felt the same way, I didn’t see it coming at all. When I found out I couldn’t tell anyone because I still wanted to make things work and wanted my family and friends to still treat her as my wife. We went to couples therapy for a long time and in the aftermath we were actually maybe the (fake?)happiest we’d been together over our whole marriage. In my eyes she was acting the way I always wanted my partner to act, thinking about me and my needs as much as her own for what seemed to me to be the first time since we were first dating. It was glorious, though I still had a lot of intrusive thoughts about ‘the incident’. But she was attentive to that and it felt really good to have someone looking out for me. The decline. Over the next year or so things steadily declined. She moved back towards selfishness and I once again felt something big missing in my life. I don’t blame her for the way she approached our relationship it’s just that we learned different ideas about it from our upbringing. And neither of us were happy. We continued therapy and really fuckin tried but eventually decided together that it was best for us to separate. Today. We’re splitting time at our house with the kids. I’m staying with my family while I’m not here and she’s staying with hers when I am. The kids are doing fine mostly. 7yo still doesn’t quite get it but she’s okay. But holy hell I’m lonely. I have friends I see regularly which is great and my family has been amazing but I love being in love and goddamn I miss it. And it’s fucking scary. I’ve yet to join hinge or any other dating app…the last time I dated was in 2006 and my confidence has been chipped away for the last 20 years. I’m sure I’ll get there but I wish I felt more inclined so I could more easily move to the next phase. I’m not really interested in casual hookups but maybe that’s the answer I have no idea. This might die without anyone reading and that’s okay. Nobody other than my therapist knows about the cheating so anyone who reads this will be in a select group. lol. Feels good to get it out. Thanks to anyone who got this far. Love to all.
item.dc:creator: TCooley79

item.title: Protip: Don't get coffee from a place called HomeWreckers.

item.title: GovBrief Today #471
item.description: Bill Cassidy finished third in his own Senate primary tonight for voting to convict Trump in the January 6 impeachment trial. It's the second Trump-driven primary purge in twelve days, with a third already teed up. Five Indiana Republican state senators lost their seats May 5 for refusing to redistrict on Trump's timeline. Representative Thomas Massie will likely lose his Kentucky primary Tuesday, and tonight Trump threatened to do the same to Lauren Boebert for campaigning in Kentucky with Massie. Pay no attention to the fact that the filing deadline for Boebert's seat has already passed. This administration finds a way around rules like that. Marjorie Taylor Greene and Boebert defined MAGA in the House. Trump still drove Greene out and turned her into a target. Boebert is next. The Bush, Cheney, and Romney families couldn't stop MAGA when they had the chance. Remind the Republicans in your life that to save what's left of their party, they need to step up now. I've emailed 7,436 stories over 471 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Don't miss today's quote of the day, images, and data on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260516-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-may16 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/ford-home [3] https://go.govbrief.today/ice-plates [4] https://go.govbrief.today/cassidy-loses [5] https://go.govbrief.today/ballroom-budget [6] https://go.govbrief.today/boebert-threat [7] https://go.govbrief.today/fema-gap [8] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-posting [9] https://go.govbrief.today/alabama-protests Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Random facts about Middle-Earth that didn't make it into the Lord of the Rings movies
item.description:
1. The elves were hella tall In canon, Galadriel was 6'4" (193 cm). In fact, it was rare for elf-women to be under six feet tall. When it comes to the elven-fellas, meanwhile, it wasn't unusual for them to hit seven feet (~214cm) -- in fact that was how tall Elrond was. The tallest elf who ever lived, King Thingol of Doriath (who lived thousands of years before the events of the Lord of the Rings) was well over eight feet tall, possibly as tall as 9 feet (244cm-274cm).
2. Aragorn and his people were also really tall At one point in the movie, Eowyn realizes that Aragorn is way older than he looks -- 87 years old -- marking him as one of the Dunedain, a race of humans "blessed with long life. Other than that, he looks like anyone else. However, in the books, Tolkien makes it clear that Aragorn's people had been literally blessed by the One God, Eru Illuvatar, with a physique much closer to that of the elves than regular humans. Not only did they live much longer than the normies, they were also way taller. In the books, Aragorn was around 6'6" (182cm). Many of the people of Gondor were, as Aragorn said, his "kin" and were therefore also tall. Boromir and Faramir were just a shade shorter than Aragorn, and it seems their tomato-scrunching dad Denethor, was a shade taller than Aragorn.
3. Galadriel had a daughter with a sad fate In the Lord of the Rings, we met Elrond's beautiful daughter Arwen, who eventually married Aragorn. But what about Arwen's mummy? Celebrían was born early in the Second Age, long before the forging of the Rings of Power -- by the events of the Lord of the Rings, she would've been thousands of years old. She had married Elrond and bore him three kids -- Arwen, and two sons (we'll talk about them later). A few centuries before the events of the Lord of the Rings, Celebrían was crossing the mountains to visit her mother when her group was attacked by orcs. Everyone thought she was dead, but her sons hunted for her ceaselessly, discovering she'd been kidnapped alive by the orcs. They rescued her and brought her home to Elrond. Celebrían had suffered horrendously at the hands of the orcs, and while Elrond was able to heal her body, he could not cure the psychological effects of her trauma. She had lost all hope in Middle-Earth, so she boarded a ship and went to Tolkien's version of heaven (where Gandalf and the others go at the end of the movies).
4. Elrond had twin sons, Elladan and Elrohir As far as I'm aware, Tolkien never specifically said they were twins, but everybody basically assumes they were. They were a bit older than Arwen. They were excellent warriors, and scouts in the wild. As previously mentioned they rescued their mum from orcs, and that experience gave them a lifelong hatred of orcs. They fought side by side with Aragorn's forebears, and with Aragorn himself, and in fact one of them trod the Paths of the Dead alongside Aragorn. Both brothers were at the Battle of the Plennor Fields at the walls of Minas Tirith, as well the final battle at the Black Gate of Mordor. I know, they were with Aragorn at these key moments, but were deleted from the movies! However, in the books, they don't say or do much, they're just sort of ... there. After the events of the movies, they stuck around for awhile, living in their dad's house. It's not known what their eventual fate was -- whether they boarded a boat and joined their parents, or chose a mortal life and died.
5. People who were "half elven" could chose to be elf OR human Elrond had a twin brother named Elros. After the gods/angels/whatever finally intervened and defeated the original Dark Lord Morgoth (Sauron was a lieutenant of Morgoth), Elrond and Elros were given a choice. They had a human father and an elf mother. They could choose to be an elf, thus living forever, or they could join the people of their father and become human. Elrond (of course) chose the immortal life, while his brother Elros stuck with the humans. Elros was given an extremely long lifespan of centuries, but he still was mortal -- yet Elros became the founding king of a great dynasty of the blessed humans of whom Aragorn was a descendant (yes, Arwen and Aragorn were technically related). The same choice was given to the children of Elrond, which is why Arwen was able to make that choice and stick with the humans because she loved Aragorn so much. When he eventually died, she left Gondor and surrendered her life out of grief (not exactly suicide, more like dying of a broken heart).
6. Mortality was originally considered a "gift" to humans from the One God Everybody dies, right? Well, not the elves. Us miserable humans sometimes dream of living forever, but in the universe created by J. R. R. Tolkien, the immortality of elves is just the way elves live. They don't think they're special or superior because of it. In fact, it's not exactly immortality; basically, their bodies are tied to the earth, and as the earth ages they do too. Instead, they feel exhausted, tired, and struggle with a sense of sadness and futility. When elves die, their spirits leave Middle-Earth and go to a kind of palace in heaven, where they wait. The length of time they wait there is dependent on what kind of lives they led (bad life, long wait; good life, short wait). Eventually their spirits are re-housed in new bodies. What happens to humans after they die? Nobody knows. It's a huge mystery. Not even the quasi-gods who manage the universe know. Wherever humans go, they never come back (with extremely rare exceptions). That mortality was a gift bestowed upon humans by the One God. It meant that humans could live lives of passion and meaning, and would never have to face the dispiriting reality of elven immortality. In fact, elves envied humans, and for a time, humans were grateful for this "gift." For centuries, in their wonderful kingdom of Numenor (basically the Atlantis of Middle Earth), the ancestors of Aragorn didn't mind mortality one bit. In fact, the kings would live their long lives, and when they started feeling the first hint of old age, they handed over the kingdom to their sons, lay down and voluntarily died. Over time, the will of the Dark Lord, which remained in the world even after he was kicked out of the universe, influenced the minds of humans, who became envious of the immortality of the elves. They stopped laying down their lives voluntarily, choosing to die of old age (experiencing all the shitty consequences that accompanies that). In fact, Aragorn was the first human in many centuries to voluntarily end his life, dying before old age hit him, at the age of 210.
7. Gandalf, Saruman, Sauron and balrogs were all the same "species" Okay. At the very top is Eru Illuvitar, the One god, who's distant and unknowable. Below Eru are the Valar, who are godlike beings of extraordinary power who served as Eru's instruments in creating and running the univers. And below the Valar are the Maiar. These are angelic beings who are kind of "helpers" of different Valar. So a maia could, for example, be associated with Aulë the Smith, or with Irmo, the Master of Visions and Dreams. Early on there was a split among these divine beings, with Melkor, the most powerful Valar, rejected everybody and tried to create life according to his own will and vision. He brought along Maiar of his own, and managed to win over Maiar who served other Valar. All these divine beings possessed within them the Flame Imperishable (the "Flame of Anor" that Gandalf referenced in the movies). They couldn't extinguish that flame, but they could distort it. Some of those Maiar twisted it to turn their forms into that of a pure, ultimate warrior. That's where the balrogs came from. They concentrated their essence into this warrior form to such an extreme degree that, while they were immortal, upon their deaths their spirits were drastically reduced to the point where they were little more than grumpy, impotent spirits wandering the earth (this happened to Sauron too). In heaven, Saruman and Sauron served the same Valar, so they must have known each other. Gandalf served the lord of visions and dreams, but also loved the goddess of living things, and thus was a gentle, compassionate person. Sauron concentrated all his power into the One Ring. Gandalf still had all his divine power, but was forbidden to use it in Middle Earth, existing instead in the body of an old man (though he kind of let the power peek out occasionally).
8. The origin of orcs is a bit fuzzy Tolkien never quite settled on the origin story of the orcs. An important part of his mythology was that Morgoth, the Dark Lord, rebelled against the One God because he wanted to create life in his own vision -- but discovering that he couldn't create life (only the One God could do that), he instead took existing life forms and twisted/perverted it. Therefore, the prevailing theory of the origin of orcs is that Morgoth captured elves, tortured the bejesus out of them, maybe other twisted crap too, and made them into these icky, twisted creatures that hated everyone and everything, including themselves. It's also possible that humans were the ones twisted and mutilated, or that Morgoth somehow managed to create them out of goo and slime and who knows what (the movies showed Saruman doing this). If orcs came from elves, that raises the possibility they inherited the immortality of the elves, but it seems that orcs have short, miserable-ass lives. It is known that orcs reproduce sexually (like humans and elves), which means there are female orcs -- but female orcs are never shown in the movies or the books.
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.dc:creator: Doomgriever

item.title: Cue the choir of angels singing, “Hallelujah!”
item.description:
#memes #socially_awkward_awesome_penguin Yesterday’s post: https://imgur.com/gallery/i-did-all-cutting-of-fat-rubbed-harder-than-me-alone-on-friday-night-with-internet-S3joUed So, I smoked the brisket and it went well. I was surprised. I did have a problem. I thought the pellets I was using was fine. However, they crumbled later in the day. This caused a clog in my auger. I then took my shop vac and sucked out all the pellets, unclogged the augur and got fresh pellets that didn’t crumble. I did wrap it at 150 instead of 165 as with the problem with the smoker I didn’t want to dry it out. An imgurian gave me advice and I did spritz it a few times with water to keep it moist as I was fixing my Traeger. In the end, I got it to 205 degrees. I smeared tallow and butter on the aluminum foil and placed it back on. I had a cooler so I wrapped it in a bunch of kitchen towels and let it rest for 1.5 hours. In the end, it jiggled and was moist!
Here it is after about an hour of smoking!
This was around 4 hours of smoking. I took the fat off and wanted to take a picture.
The lid kept coming up so I used water to hold it down.
The result!!! Look at that bark.
I did look up how to cut the meat!
Look at that. So delicious. I did think it over on what I would have done differently. I would have spritz more, and let it rest in the oven instead with the towels so it was even. It is so amazing and delicious. I wasn’t a fan of brisket until now.
Dog tax. Felix wants me to play fetch now. Yes, he got a little bit. I’m no monster. See you all later, you beautiful bastards!
item.dc:creator: Hotsauceman

item.title: Uzbekistani Gymnast Dilnoza Abdusalimova Serving Real-Life JoJo Energy
item.dc:creator: Sleave34
